title: Looks like tracing_off does not work with Hierarchy blocks?

features.name: tracing_off with hier_block directive
features.description: Code should include a tracing_off directive combined with a hier_block declaration targeting a specific module

features.name: enum typedef for state machine
features.description: Code should include a typedef enum with explicit values used as operation type
features.snippet: typedef enum logic [1:0] { add = 2'h1, sub = 2'h2, nop = 2'h0 } operation_t /*verilator public*/;

features.name: module with enum port interface
features.description: Code should include a module with an enum-typed input port for operation selection
features.snippet: module alu #(parameter WIDTH = 6) (input clk, input rst_n, input operation_t op_in, input [WIDTH-1:0] a_in, input [WIDTH-1:0] b_in, input in_valid, output logic [WIDTH-1:0] out, output logic out_valid);

features.name: registered inputs with async reset
features.description: Code should include input registers in always_ff with asynchronous reset
features.snippet: always_ff @ (posedge clk, posedge rst_n) begin if (rst_n) begin op_in_r <= nop; a_in_r <= '0; in_valid_r <= '0; end else begin op_in_r <= op_in; a_in_r <= a_in; in_valid_r <= in_valid; end end

features.name: combinational logic with case and arithmetic
features.description: Code should include always_comb with case statement performing addition and subtraction operations
features.snippet: always_comb begin result = '0; if (in_valid_r) begin case (op_in_r) add: result = a_in_r + b_in_r; sub: result = a_in_r + (~b_in_r+1'b1); default: result = '0; endcase end end

features.name: multi-module instantiation with named ports
features.description: Code should include a top module instantiating multiple submodules with named port connections
features.snippet: module top(input clk, input rst_n, input en, output reg [15:0] led, input operation_t op_in, input [5:0] a_in, input [5:0] b_in, input in_valid, output logic [5:0] out, output logic out_valid); alu u_alu(.clk, .rst_n, .op_in, .a_in, .b_in, .in_valid, .out, .out_valid); led u_led(.clk, .rst_n, .en, .led); endmodule
